if the scanned sub-sequence of {@code csq}

PARAM.description: Note: When implementing an escaper it is a good idea to override this method for efficiency by inlining the implementation of {@link #nextEscapeIndex(CharSequence, int, int)} directly. Doing this for {@link com.google.common.net.PercentEscaper} more than doubled the performance for unescaped strings (as measured by {@link CharEscapersBenchmark}).
PARAM.name: string
PARAM.content:
the literal string to be escaped

If you are escaping input in arbitrary successive chunks, then it is not generally safe to use this method. If an input string ends with an unmatched high surrogate character, then this method will throw {@link IllegalArgumentException}. You should ensure your input is valid UTF-16 before calling this method.

RETURN.description: the escaped form of the literal string.
RETURN.content: <pre>If you are escaping input in arbitrary successive chunks, then it is not generally safe to use this method. If an input string ends with an unmatched high surrogate character, then this method will throw IllegalArgumentException.</pre>

THROWS.description: if invalid surrogate characters are encountered
THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content: if invalid surrogate characters are encountered

PARAM.name: index
PARAM.content: the index to start escaping from must be of type int

RETURN.description: Returns the escaped form of a given literal string, starting at the given index.
RETURN.content: Returns the escaped form of a given literal string, starting at the given index.

THROWS.name: IllegalArgumentException
THROWS.content:
if invalid surrogate characters are
        encountered
THROWS.description:
if invalid surrogate characters are
        encountered

PARAM.description: the sequence must not be null
PARAM.name: seq

PARAM.description: end must be greater than or equal to index
PARAM.name: end
PARAM.content: <strong>End must be greater than or equal to index</strong>

RETURN.description: The Unicode code point for the given index.
RETURN.assertion: assert result == ((int) (seq.charAt(index)) || (index >= end && seq.charCodeAt(end-1)) ? seq.charCodeAt(end-1)-0x110000 : seq.codePointAt(index, end));
RETURN.content: The Unicode code point for the given index.
